scenarios.label: Appalachian clear tailwater, winter control month 1

expectation.seasonal_story: A clear Appalachian winter tailwater should stay subtle and lower-column with sculpin, bugger, leech, and disciplined hair support.

color_decision.short_reason: Clear water + bright sun — go natural.

top_1_lure.why_chosen: This is today's top overall read. It matches the column/pace the conditions push toward today. With the highest lane shut down, this keeps you just under the cleaner active zone. That fits the slower worm-and-leech food profile this window still allows.
top_1_lure.how_to_fish: Cast across, let it sink on a controlled slack line, then swim it back with short pauses so the marabou pulses and collapses. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

color_decision.short_reason: Clear water + bright sun — go natural.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: This is today's top overall read. It matches the column/pace the conditions push toward today. With the highest lane shut down, this keeps you just under the cleaner active zone. That fits the slower worm-and-leech food profile this window still allows.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Cast across, let it sink on a controlled slack line, then swim it back with short pauses so the marabou pulses and collapses. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

color_decision.short_reason: Clear water + bright sun — go natural.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: Behind today's lead look, this stays inside the day's window. It matches the column/pace the conditions push toward today. With the highest lane shut down, this keeps you just under the cleaner active zone. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window. It rounds out the lineup with a different look. It gives you a different reaction mid column look without leaving today's window.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Vertical jig it with short, sharp hops over suspended fish; the flutter-fall is the presentation — set hard the moment you feel weight. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

color_decision.short_reason: Clear water + bright sun — go natural.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: Rounding out the trio as a change-up angle. It matches the column/pace the conditions push toward today. With the highest lane shut down, this keeps you just under the cleaner active zone. Paddle-Tail Swimbait stays in play when baitfish is relevant. It rounds out the lineup with a different look. It is the cleaner change-up if the lead look does not convert.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Slow-roll near the bottom on a consistent retrieve; the paddle thumps best just barely above the substrate, so keep the rod tip down and the pace even. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

color_decision.short_reason: Clear water + bright sun — go natural.

top_1_fly.why_chosen: This is today's top overall read. It matches the column/pace the conditions push toward today. With the highest lane shut down, this keeps you just under the cleaner active zone. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_1_fly.how_to_fish: Let it sink to depth then retrieve with slow hand-twists or short strips; the marabou tail breathes best with long pauses at the end of each pull. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

color_decision.short_reason: Clear water + bright sun — go natural.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: This is today's top overall read. It matches the column/pace the conditions push toward today. With the highest lane shut down, this keeps you just under the cleaner active zone. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Let it sink to depth then retrieve with slow hand-twists or short strips; the marabou tail breathes best with long pauses at the end of each pull. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

color_decision.short_reason: Clear water + bright sun — go natural.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: Behind today's lead look, this stays inside the day's window. It matches the column/pace the conditions push toward today. It stays practical in current seams and river lanes when flow still matters. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window. It rounds out the lineup with a different look. It gives you a different fly bottom look without leaving today's window.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Cast upstream and mend for a natural drift, then activate with short strips that make the fly bumble over the bottom like a sculpin retreating. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

color_decision.short_reason: Clear water + bright sun — go natural.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: Rounding out the trio as a change-up angle. It matches the column/pace the conditions push toward today. It stays practical in current seams and river lanes when flow still matters. That fits the slower worm-and-leech food profile this window still allows. It rounds out the lineup with a different look. It is the cleaner change-up if the lead look does not convert.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Cast across or down, let it sink to depth, then strip with a jig-like cadence where the cone tips the fly nose-down on each pause. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window. It rounds out the lineup with a different look. It is the cleaner change-up if the lead look does not convert.

color_decision.short_reason: Clear water + low light — a darker profile reads best.

top_1_fly.why_chosen: This is today's top overall read. It matches the column/pace the conditions push toward today. Lower light supports a slightly higher, more open lane. The month is still baitfish-forward, and this stays inside that search lane.
top_1_fly.how_to_fish: Strip in short, consistent pulls so the dumbbell eyes make the fly jig up and down; let it sink between strips to stay hook-point up throughout.

color_decision.short_reason: Clear water + low light — a darker profile reads best.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: This is today's top overall read. It matches the column/pace the conditions push toward today. Lower light supports a slightly higher, more open lane. The month is still baitfish-forward, and this stays inside that search lane.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Strip in short, consistent pulls so the dumbbell eyes make the fly jig up and down; let it sink between strips to stay hook-point up throughout.

color_decision.short_reason: Clear water + low light — a darker profile reads best.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: Behind today's lead look, this stays inside the day's window. It matches the column/pace the conditions push toward today. Lower light supports a slightly higher, more open lane. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window. It gives you a different fly baitfish look without leaving today's window.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Retrieve with a mix of short darts and longer glides; the thin profile tracks like a small minnow on a tight line. Keep it high in the zone.

color_decision.short_reason: Clear water + low light — a darker profile reads best.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: Rounding out the trio as a change-up angle. It matches the column/pace the conditions push toward today. Lower light supports a slightly higher, more open lane. The month is still baitfish-forward, and this stays inside that search lane. It is the cleaner change-up if the lead look does not convert.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Cast across or down and retrieve with consistent strips; add a pause every 4-5 strips so the bucktail flares and collapses like a stunned baitfish. Keep it high in the zone.
